vulnerability.reason: generated describes lack of safeguards instead of engineers' assumption of stable performance
vulnerability.reference: Engineers assume stable performance across tasks after fine-tuning.
vulnerability.generated: Fine-tuned video-LLMs lack built-in safeguards against error amplification, leaving researchers unable to detect cascading misinterpretations.

impact.reason: both affected party (downstream applications) and damage type (untrustworthy failures) are present
impact.reference: Legacy task failures propagate unnoticed into downstream applications.
impact.generated: Model reliability collapses across tasks, producing untrustworthy video analyses that invalidate research outcomes and downstream applications.

dose_response.reason: generated arc describes error amplification rather than catastrophic forgetting of legacy tasks
dose_response.reference: Continuous attention and sticky memories in long video models enable catastrophic forgetting, exposing fine-tuning engineers to legacy task failures that propagate into unreliable downstream applications and trust decay.
dose_response.generated: Continuous attention and sticky memories amplify errors and cascading misinterpretations, through continual fine-tuning, triggers the lack of safeguards that leaves these misinterpretations undetected, leading to collapsed model reliability and untrustworthy analyses invalidating research outcomes.
